title: Reference-Aligned Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering over Heterogeneous Proprietary Documents
abstract: This paper addresses challenges in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for domain expert Question Answering (QA) systems in low-resource domains with heterogeneous data formats. The authors present a RAG development pipeline applied to automotive engineering, specifically for vehicle crash collision test QA. Their approach includes a data generation pipeline for internal automotive company documents, an advanced re-ranking phase, and a reference-matching algorithm to enhance accuracy and traceability.

methodology.focus_domain: Automotive engineering, specifically vehicle crash collision tests
key_components: Data generation pipeline for internal automotive company documents
methodology.system_architecture: Retrieves and re-ranks text chunks, generates answers using top-k relevant chunks, and ensures each part of the answer is backed by clear references

challenges_addressed: Heterogeneous data formats in low-resource domains
Data privacy and security concerns restricting API-based LLMs
Limited research on improving ranking in the retrieval phase
Inadequate evaluation metrics for RAG systems
results: The system retrieves and re-ranks text chunks, generates answers using top-k relevant chunks, and ensures each part of the answer is backed by clear references. The example shown was originally in Korean and translated into English.
goal: To create a fully local system ensuring data privacy and independence
citation: This work addresses three key challenges in RAG systems and presents a development pipeline with application in the automotive engineering domain.
